key_insight: PPI-GD reduces gradient oracle calls by interpolating gradient samples over patches of the data domain, exploiting smoothness in the training data rather than only in the optimization variables.
engineering_relevance: This suggests faster large-scale ERM training when data-domain smoothness is available, especially in regimes where standard gradient descent is oracle-expensive.
method: Query first-order oracle at equidistant data points
Build d-variate tensor-product polynomial interpolants on local patches
Use interpolated gradients inside an inexact gradient descent scheme
evidence_signal: The abstract reports oracle-complexity analysis for strongly convex and non-convex losses and shows improved scaling over several GD variants when the loss is sufficiently smooth and data dimension is only polylogarithmic in the sample size.
watch_item: Performance depends on strong smoothness assumptions and a bounded data dimension, so interpolation error and patch construction may limit practical gains in high-dimensional or noisy settings.
paper_title: On the Oracle Complexity of Interpolation-Based Gradient Descent
